sub_questions.question: Who originally made the claim that the Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed to US/Israel-linked vessels, or is @currentreport1 the original source?
What specifically does 'effectively closed' mean in the context of the Strait of Hormuz for US/Israel-linked vessels? Does it imply a physical blockade, a threat, or a policy change?
When was this claim made or reported by @currentreport1 or any other primary source?
Where was this claim originally reported or stated by an official source, if any, and what was the original context?

Is there any official statement or document from relevant authorities (e.g., Iran, US, Israel, international maritime organizations) regarding restrictions on vessels in the Strait of Hormuz for US/Israel-linked vessels?
